title: papualthia mollis
intro: Papualthia mollis, also known as the Malabar neem or Malabar neem tree, is a species of plant in the mahogany family that is native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal agent. The health benefits of Papualthia mollis include its use in treating various skin conditions, such as acne, wounds, and dermatitis, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of Papualthia mollis include leaves and bark extracts that can be used to make teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
